  "findings": [
    "No thermal expansion tank installed on a closed water supply system."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install an expansion tank on the cold water supply line to the water heater to accommodate thermal expansion."
  ],
  "rationale": "A closed plumbing system (e.g., one with a backflow preventer, check valve, or pressure-reducing valve) requires a thermal expansion control device. Without it, thermal expansion during heating cycles will cause excessive pressure spikes, potentially damaging the water heater, piping, or causing the T&P relief valve to discharge continuously.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 504",
    "IRC P2804"
  ]
}
